summary:
Claim: Fibroblasts are the predominant connective tissue cells in the periodontal ligament.  
Evidence: - Fibroblasts are numerically dominant and responsible for synthesizing collagen and other ECM components. - They play a central role in maintaining structural integrity and enabling repair via dynamic matrix turnover.  
Answer: B

The claim is accurate because fibroblasts form the majority of cell types in the periodontal ligament and perform essential functions related to extracellular matrix production and maintenance. Key evidence includes their numerical dominance—making up over 90% of all cells—and their active participation in both normal physiological function and pathological responses such as inflammation and injury. These roles underscore their centrality in the biomechanical support provided by the periodontal ligament. While cementoblasts (which produce dentin) and osteoblasts (which form bone) exist nearby, they reside primarily in adjacent structures rather than within the main body of the PDL. Therefore, despite some overlap in tissue boundaries, these cells are not considered part of the principal cellular composition of the periodontal ligament proper.

Final answer: B

Key evidence includes their numerical dominance—making up over 90% of all cells—and their active participation in both normal physiological function and pathological responses such as inflammation and injury.
